Voice-First Start Page

Navable
Welcome back.
--:--
Settings

Open a place, ask a question, or let the page guide itself.

Navable keeps the opening screen quieter: fewer distractions, cleaner navigation cues, and a faster route into what you actually need.

Voice entry

Talk first when that is faster

Not listening.
“Open YouTube” “Open example dot com” “What is photosynthesis?”

Jump in

Launch a destination

Keep a few clean starting points one tap away.

Useful cues

Stay oriented without clutter

  • Use the popup when you want summaries, headings, or focused-element feedback.
  • Press Alt+Shift+M any time to toggle voice listening.
  • Use Chrome shortcuts when you want repeatable demo flows.

Try saying

Natural openings

  • “Open YouTube”
  • “Read title”
  • “Summarize this page”

Why it feels cleaner

One screen, three jobs

Open a site, ask a short question, or start listening, without stacking five unrelated widgets.

Built for demos

Keep the story obvious

The layout stays readable at a glance so the voice interaction remains the headline, not the chrome.

Press Alt+Shift+M to toggle voice. · Open settings
